scriptId: cs_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
historyId: h_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Excessive phone or social media use

goal: Reduce evening phone use by 75% within six weeks; track evening phone minutes per day as the primary metric.
constraints: Non-confrontational boundary setting with a cohabitant; focus on personal needs; avoid shaming or lecturing.
decisions: Adopt non-time metrics tracking: impulse pickups, urge intensity, evening mood, and sleep quality as core metrics.

Adopt a notification-management plan: audit top noisy apps, categorize notifications into Essential/Important/Low Priority, configure per-app settings (lock screen, banners, sounds, vibration; quiet delivery), leverage Focus/Do Not Disturb modes with exceptions, enable emergency bypass, batch checks, and roll out gradually.
Evaluate and document reliable built-in features and free apps for enforcing limits; identify least-beatable configurations and compile step-by-step setup.
Convert the six-week targets into exact per-app timers and a one-page checklist to implement across devices (iPhone/Android).
Expand boundary communication: develop short scripts for friends/family to explain slower responses.
Implement work windows concept: designate specific time blocks for work-related app activity and silence outside those windows.
Separate endpoints for work vs personal use: use desktop/web for networking while mobile is kept for quick messages only.
Configure per-app notification strategies to allow messages from VIP contacts only and mute feed/algorithmic notifications during work windows.
Introduce a read-later/batch content workflow: save posts to a read-later queue and review during scheduled windows.
Create a 2-3 daily networking window schedule (e.g., 20-30 min post-lunch, 30-40 min evening) with timers and track outputs (messages replied, leads, follow-ups).
Use 'message-only workflows': direct messaging as primary interface for work; avoid starting from main feed; prepare ready replies.
Improve UI placement: place networking apps in a folder or secondary screen to require intentional step to open; optionally log out of feed to add friction.
Leverage desktop for deep work with feed-blocking extensions.
Adopt journaling cadence: daily micro-check-ins and weekly reflections using the provided prompts and templates.
Incorporate urge-management scripts and delay techniques into the plan.
Integrate a mid-evening fallback plan module into the plan; develop a quick-reference module with steps and timers.
Test and document the fallback plan efficacy during a 1-week trial; record misses and adjustments.
Add quick-reference urge-management script for use during relapses (Not now — pause for 15 minutes; walk and reassess).
entities: short/inconvenient charging cable to add friction
shared charging bowl and nonverbal time cues
Daily journaling cadence (30-60 seconds) and weekly reflection prompts
open: Add a boundary-setting module for home cohabitants to the plan, including no-phone hour and a phone bowl routine to test with a partner for one week.
Draft simple, non-confrontational conversation scripts (I statements) to propose the boundary to a cohabitant.
Create a brief, testable no-phone living-room rule (e.g., 30–60 minutes) and monitor impact on calm and focus.
Test and implement notification-management plan across devices for a one-week trial; document misses and adjustments.
Evaluate and implement hard-to-beat enforcement setup across iPhone/Android; draft setup guide and timelines.
Generate per-app timer configurations and a one-page checklist for Week 1-6 to paste into Notes for iPhone and Android.
Develop social-context boundary signaling templates for stepping back in non-cohabitants conversations (casual, close, acquaintance, group chat, and work).
Define and implement 'work windows' across devices: schedule 2-3 focused networking blocks daily; ensure desktop/mobile separation.
Configure and test per-app notification rules: VIP-only messaging during work windows; mute feed outside windows.
Set up a read-later workflow: install Pocket/Instapaper or use built-in read-later; creating a review slot.
Create a dedicated workflow: 'message-only' approach for work—open messages directly, not feed.
Organize networking apps into a dedicated folder/secondary screen; require deliberate step to access; optional logout to add friction.
Establish a weekly audit of algorithmic recommendations and autoplay settings; disable where possible.
Test and document physical-adjustment plan (phone parking spots, charging basket, out-of-reach placement) across home; monitor impact on calm and focus for one week.
Implement journaling protocol: establish daily micro-check-ins and weekly reflections; document results and adjust cadence as needed.
Design and implement a rewards-based reinforcement plan: define tokens, tracking method, and redemption options.
Create a quick-reference rewards guide listing approved immediate rewards, values, and redemption thresholds.
Integrate the rewards plan with boundary and work-window strategies to ensure coherence.
Create a quick-reference urge-management script guide and test in real use.

Create an emergency-contact balance protocol: outline how to keep one emergency contact reachable while limiting everything else; include device-specific Focus/Do Not Disturb overrides, testing plan, and a shareable quick-reference script for the contact.
Integrate a mid-evening fallback plan module into the plan; develop a quick-reference module with steps and timers.
Pilot test the fallback plan for one week and report results.
Add quick-reference urge-management script for use during relapses (Not now — pause for 15 minutes; walk and reassess).
